title: Is a College Degree Still Worth It
subtitle: 大学文凭还值钱吗？教育回报正在被重新定价

paragraphs.en: As the latest crop of students pen their undergraduate application form and weigh up their options, it may be worth considering just how the point, purpose and value of a degree has changed and what Generation Z need to consider as they start the third stage of their educational journey. Millennials were told that if you did well in school, got a decent degree, you would be set up for life. But that promise has been found wanting.
paragraphs.zh: 当最新一批学生填写本科申请表、权衡各种选择时，或许值得思考一下：学位的意义、目的和价值究竟发生了怎样的变化，Z世代在开启教育旅程的第三阶段时又需要考虑什么。千禧一代曾被告知，只要在校成绩优异、拿到一份体面的学位，人生就有了保障。然而，这一承诺已被证明不尽如人意。

paragraphs.en: As degrees became universal, they became devalued. Education was no longer a secure route of social mobility. Today, 28 percent of graduates in the UK are in non-graduate roles, a percentage which is double the average among OECD countries.
paragraphs.zh: 随着大学学位变得普及，其价值也随之缩水。教育不再是通往社会流动的稳妥通道。如今，英国28%的毕业生从事着无需本科学历的工作，这一比例是经合组织国家平均水平的两倍。

paragraphs.en: This is not to say that there is no point in getting a degree, but rather stress that a degree is not for everyone, that the switch from classroom to lecture hall is not an inevitable one and that other options are available. Thankfully, there are signs that this is already happening, with Generation Z seeking to learn from their millennial predecessors, even if parents and teachers tend to be still set in the degree mindset.
paragraphs.zh: 这并非说读大学毫无意义，而是强调学位并不适合所有人，从中学课堂到大学讲厅的过渡并非必然之选，还有其他路径可走。所幸已有迹象表明这种转变正在发生——Z世代正努力向千禧一代的前辈们学习，即便家长和老师往往仍固守着学历至上的思维定式。

paragraphs.en: Employers have long seen the advantages of hiring school leavers who often prove themselves to be more committed and loyal employees than graduates. Many too are seeing the advantages of scrapping a degree requirement for certain roles. For those for whom a degree is the desired route, consider that this may well be the first of many.
paragraphs.zh: 雇主们早已发现聘用中学毕业生的好处，这些人往往比大学毕业生更投入、更忠诚。许多雇主也意识到取消某些岗位的学历门槛有其优势。对那些本就希望走大学这条路的人来说，不妨意识到这可能只是众多阶段中的第一个。

paragraphs.en: In this age of generalists, it pays to have specific knowledge or skills. Postgraduates now earn 40 percent more than graduates. When more and more of us have a degree, it makes sense to have two.
paragraphs.zh: 在这个通才遍地的时代，拥有专门的知识或技能才更具回报。如今，研究生的收入比本科生高出40%。当拥有本科学位的人越来越多时，拿两个学位也就变得合情合理了。

paragraphs.en: It is unlikely that Generation Z will be done with education at 18 or 21: they will need to be constantly upskilling throughout their career to stay employable. It has been estimated that this generation, due to the pressures of technology, the wish for personal fulfillment and desire for diversity will work for 17 different employers over the course of their working life and have five different careers. Education, and not just knowledge gained on campus, will be a core part of Generation Z's career trajectory.
paragraphs.zh: Z世代不太可能在18岁或21岁就彻底告别教育：他们需要在职业生涯中不断更新技能，才能保持就业竞争力。据估计，由于技术压力、对自我实现的追求以及对多样性的渴望，这一代人一生中会为17个不同的雇主工作，经历五种不同的职业。教育，而不仅是在校园里学到的知识，将成为Z世代职业发展轨迹的核心组成部分。

paragraphs.en: Older generations often talk about their degree in the present and personal tense: ‘I am a geographer' or ‘I am a classist'. Their sons or daughters would never say such a thing; it's as if they already know that their degree won't define them in the same way.
paragraphs.zh: 老一辈人谈论自己的学位时，常用现在时且带有强烈的个人认同感：“我是一名地理学者”或“我是一名古典学者”。而他们的儿女绝不会说这样的话，仿佛他们早已明白，学位不会再以同样的方式定义自己的人生。
